headline: Is AVGO Stock Expensive At $440?
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) stock currently trades at $440, positioning it as a pivotal force in the global shift toward AI-driven infrastructure. At a 38.5x forward P/E based on 2026 earnings, the valuation appears rich compared to the average figure of 34.8x over recent quarters. However, shifting the lens to a three-year horizon through 2028 reveals a massive earnings power that the market is currently attempting to price in today.

content.title: Is AVGO Stock Expensive At $440?
content.summary: Broadcom (AVGO) stock currently trades at $440, positioning it as a pivotal force in the global shift toward AI-driven infrastructure. At a 38.5x forward P/E based on 2026 earnings, the valuation appears rich compared to the average figure of 34.8x over recent quarters. However, shifting the lens to a three-year horizon through 2028 reveals a massive earnings power that the market is currently attempting to price in today.

headline: Trump Bought the Software Stock Slump—and More Revelations From His Trades
summary: President Donald Trump has been busy in the stock market.  Trump disclosed purchases and sales of hundreds of millions of dollars worth of U.S. stocks in the first quarter, according to a disclosure released by the U.S. Office of Government Ethics.  Trump purchased between $1 million and $5 million worth of  ServiceNow Adobe Workday  on Feb. 10, the disclosure revealed.

headline: Broadcom vs Microsoft. Both Are Winning the AI Race. Only One Is Priced Like It
summary: Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) both delivered AI-fueled quarters revealing two different playbooks. Broadcom sells custom silicon and switches for hyperscaler data centers. Microsoft sells cloud, copilots, and the productivity layer on top. Comparing them shows how the AI buildout splits between picks-and-shovels suppliers and full-stack platform owners. Custom Silicon Surges While Azure Carries Microsoft ... Broadcom vs Microsoft.
